framing: Senior GNC engineer at Lockheed Martin applying to SpaceX Starlink constellation role

jd.title: Staff GNC Engineer — Satellite Constellation
jd.style_note: Startup-technical: fast-paced, specific technical requirements, mission-driven, high ownership

requirements.text: 8+ years of experience in guidance, navigation, and control (GNC) for spacecraft or satellite systems
Expert knowledge of orbit mechanics, attitude determination and control systems (ADCS), and orbital manoeuvre planning
Strong programming skills in Python and C++ for GNC algorithm development and simulation
Experience with high-fidelity orbital simulation tools (STK/Ansys, GMAT, or custom Monte Carlo simulations)
Experience with constellation operations: station-keeping, collision avoidance (COLA), and deorbit planning
Experience with electric propulsion systems (Hall-effect thrusters, ion engines) for orbit raising and maintenance
Familiarity with space situational awareness (SSA) and conjunction assessment (CDM processing)
Experience with real-time spacecraft operations and anomaly resolution

responsibilities: Design and implement GNC algorithms for Starlink satellite constellation (6,000+ satellites)
Develop autonomous orbit-raising, station-keeping, and collision avoidance software
Build high-fidelity simulation environments for GNC algorithm validation
Support spacecraft anomaly resolution and on-orbit troubleshooting for Starlink fleet
Collaborate with propulsion, communications, and operations teams on constellation management

expected_output.summary: GNC engineer with 10 years of experience at Lockheed Martin Space, most recently Senior Staff Engineer on the GPS III satellite programme. Led ADCS algorithm development and validation for 4 GPS III satellites (SV05-SV08). Deep expertise in attitude determination, orbit mechanics, and high-fidelity simulation using STK and MATLAB/Simulink. Experience with chemical propulsion attitude control systems. Seeking transition from government satellite programmes to commercial constellation operations.

experience.title: Senior Staff Engineer — GNC, GPS III Programme
bullets: Led ADCS algorithm development for GPS III satellites SV05-SV08: reaction wheel control laws, star tracker processing, and Earth/Sun sensor fusion
Developed high-fidelity attitude simulation environment in MATLAB/Simulink validated against on-orbit telemetry data
Designed orbit manoeuvre planning algorithms for GPS constellation slot maintenance using chemical propulsion (bipropellant thrusters)
Supported GPS III SV05 and SV06 launch campaigns and early orbit operations at Cape Canaveral
Performed Monte Carlo simulations (10,000+ runs) for ADCS performance verification and worst-case analysis

experience.title: GNC Engineer (promoted from Associate, 2018)
bullets: Developed attitude determination algorithms for SBIRS GEO satellite programme using Kalman filter-based sensor fusion
Built orbital simulation tools using STK (Systems Tool Kit) and custom Python scripts for orbit propagation and event prediction
Performed conjunction assessment analysis using JSpOC (18th Space Control Squadron) conjunction data messages (CDMs)
Supported anomaly resolution for 2 on-orbit SBIRS satellites including safe mode recovery procedures

certifications: US Security Clearance — Secret (active, TS eligible)
education: MS Aerospace Engineering Sciences — University of Colorado Boulder (2014-2016)
BS Aeronautical and Astronautical Engineering — Purdue University (2010-2014)

jd_requirements: orbit mechanics ADCS attitude determination control orbital manoeuvre
Python C++ GNC algorithm development simulation
STK Ansys GMAT Monte Carlo orbital simulation
constellation station-keeping collision avoidance COLA deorbit
electric propulsion Hall-effect thrusters ion engines
space situational awareness SSA conjunction assessment CDM
deliberate_flaws: No C++ experience — JD requires 'strong C++ skills', candidate uses MATLAB/Simulink and Python
No electric propulsion experience — all chemical propulsion (GPS III uses bipropellant, Starlink uses Hall-effect krypton thrusters)
No constellation operations experience — GPS III is single-satellite GNC, not 6,000+ satellite fleet management
No station-keeping experience for LEO constellations (GPS is MEO, very different orbit dynamics)
No collision avoidance (COLA) algorithm development (performed conjunction assessment analysis, not automated COLA)
No GMAT experience (only STK and MATLAB)
No real-time operations experience at SpaceX pace (Lockheed Martin has different cadence)
Government satellite to commercial constellation — different risk tolerance, iteration speed, and autonomy requirements
Secret clearance, not TS — SpaceX Starlink doesn't require clearance but Lockheed background may have IP restrictions
No autonomous software development (GPS III GNC is ground-commanded, Starlink is autonomous)
